observations: SCOPE/NON_GOALS UNCHANGED, CONFIRMED PROGRAMMATICALLY: reconstructed revision 0's `scope`, `non_goals`, `name`, `spec_ref`, and `estimated_lines` verbatim from this review's own round-0 transcript (round-0 review artifact .agent/qa/032-contract-review.json references the same contract text) and structurally diffed them against revision 1 on disk: `scope identical: True`, `non_goals identical: True`, `estimated_lines identical: True`, `name identical: True`, `spec_ref identical: True`.

VERIFICATION ARRAY GREW FROM 17 TO 19 ENTRIES, WITH EXACTLY 14 BYTE-IDENTICAL, UNCHANGED: extracted all 17 round-0 commands (preserved on disk from the round-0 review session) and all 19 round-1 commands and diffed each round-0 entry against its corresponding round-1 array position (shift of +2 from the old entry 9 onward, since two new entries were inserted between old entries 8 and 9): entries 1,2,3,4,7,8 (unshifted) and 9,10,12,13,14,15,16,17 (old 9,10,12,13,14,15,16,17 -> new 11,12,14,15,16,17,18,19) are BYTE-IDENTICAL to round 0 -- 14 entries total, exactly matching the coordinator's claim. Only three entries changed (old 5, old 6, old 11 -> new 5, new 6, new 13), and two are wholly new (new 9, new 10) -- nothing else in the array was touched.

FIX 1 (HIGH, old verification[11] -> new verification[13], structural NIZAM.json module-entry check) INDEPENDENTLY RE-KILLED: re-ran BOTH round-0 kill-shot scratch fixtures (built under the scratchpad, never touching the real, contracted NIZAM.json) against the new exact-list-equality logic (`mods[0].get('key_documents')==['ecosystem/README.md']`): (a) the dangling-extra-path fixture (key_documents=['ecosystem/README.md','ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md'], second path nonexistent) now correctly FAILS; (b) the extra-but-existing fixture (same key_documents list, but with a reverted `touch ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md` simulating feature 033 having landed that path in this same working tree) now ALSO correctly FAILS -- closing the exact gap the round-0 review flagged as high-severity and non-hypothetical given 033's concurrency. A third control fixture (single correct entry, key_documents==['ecosystem/README.md'] exactly) correctly PASSES, confirming the tightened check does not merely fail everything. All fixture files were scratch-only; the real NIZAM.json and ecosystem/ directory were untouched except for the single reverted `touch`/`rm` pair used for the simulation, confirmed removed via `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- ecosystem/` showing nothing.

FIX 1's anti-vacuity companion (new verification[14], byte-identical to old verification[12]) is unchanged by design: it only proves HEAD's NIZAM.json contains no `path=='ecosystem'` entry at all (a coarser, sufficient anti-vacuity proof), independent of the key_documents membership-vs-equality distinction the round-0 finding was about -- correctly does not need to change, and re-confirmed PASS on the live tree.

FIX 2 (LOW, old verification[5]/[6] -> new verification[5]/[6], version-bump check) INDEPENDENTLY RE-KILLED: re-ran the round-0 kill-shot in-memory fixture (`version: 0.0.1` with a matching `change_log` entry, never written to any repo file) against the new semver-tuple-comparison logic (`tuple(int(x) for x in m.groups()) > (0,1,0)`): now correctly FAILS (0.0.1 < 0.1.0). A genuine-bump control fixture (`version: 0.2.0` with matching change_log) correctly PASSES. The anti-vacuity companion (new verification[6]) re-run against the live `git show HEAD:ecosystem/README.md` content: correctly PASSES (i.e. the negation holds -- HEAD's 0.1.0/no-change_log stub does not satisfy the tightened check), confirming non-vacuity.

FIX 3 (unsupported claim -> new verification[9]/[10], Shipped/Planned dynamic annotation check) INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED, INCLUDING A LIVE DYNAMIC-FILESYSTEM TEST: built two disposable README fixtures -- one with all 9 protocol docs correctly annotated 'Planned' (matching the current on-disk reality, where none of the 9 numbered docs exist), one with `01_clean_state_preflight.md` incorrectly marked 'Shipped'. Against the CURRENT clean state (re-confirmed absent via `ls ecosystem/` immediately before each check): the all-Planned fixture correctly PASSES and the one-wrong-Shipped fixture correctly FAILS. Then simulated feature 033 actually landing its file (`touch ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md`, reverted immediately after with `rm -f` and confirmed via `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all -- ecosystem/` showing no residue): under that simulated post-landing state, the SAME all-Planned fixture (now stale for that one doc) correctly FLIPS TO FAIL, and the SAME one-wrong-Shipped fixture (which happened to say 'Shipped' for that doc) correctly FLIPS TO PASS -- concretely proving the check re-evaluates against the real filesystem at run time rather than a hardcoded shipped/unshipped list, exactly as design_notes.revision1_corrections claims. NOTE: during the FIRST attempt at this same live test (before the clean re-run recorded above), an initial pair of checks unexpectedly reported `01_clean_state_preflight.md` as already existing before any simulation was performed by this review -- consistent with feature 033's own generator process transiently creating that file in this same, concurrently-shared working tree during its own implementation/rework pass (033's .agent/evidence/033-verify-*.txt files, 14 of them, appeared in `git status` partway through this review session, confirming 033 is actively executing concurrently). This is an environmental observation about shared-tree concurrency, not a defect in the check: it was independently re-confirmed by immediately re-listing `ecosystem/` before each check thereafter, and the check's behavior was correct and consistent with the true filesystem state at every observation point.

FIX 3's anti-vacuity companion (new verification[10]) re-run against `git show HEAD:ecosystem/README.md` (the 11-line stub, which names none of the 9 documents): correctly PASSES (the negation holds -- HEAD does not already carry correct Shipped/Planned annotations for all 9, since it names none of them at all), confirming non-vacuity.

REGRESSION GUARD (new verification[11], byte-identical to old verification[9], design_constraint_no_dangling_references) re-confirmed unchanged and still correctly discriminates: this entry's command was not touched in revision 1 and was already proven load-bearing in the round-0 review (directory-qualified dangling token fails, bare-filename form passes); re-ran it against the live tree in this round and it still PASSES (no directory-qualified tokens in the current 12-line stub), consistent with its status as a regression-style guard, not a content-gated check.

GUARD ENTRIES RE-CONFIRMED STABLE: verification[15] (validate.sh SUMMARY), [16] (scope guard), [17] (.agent immutability guard), [18] (non-goal schema/tools byte-identical), [19] (non-goal DEBT.md byte-identical) are byte-identical to their round-0 counterparts (9 of these already probed with reverted positive-control mutations in the round-0 review) and all still PASS on the live tree in this round; no fresh mutation re-probe was performed on these five since their command text is unchanged and their round-0 load-bearing proof stands.

FULL 19-ENTRY SUITE RE-RUN: extracted and executed all 19 verification commands programmatically (subprocess, not retyped) from the live .agent/contracts/032.json against the live working tree from the repository root. Correctly FAIL pre-implementation (content not yet authored): [1] 4-keyword lifecycle, [2] 10-stage lifecycle, [5] version bump, [7] module navigation, [9] Shipped/Planned annotation, [13] structural NIZAM.json module entry -- six content-gated entries, exactly the expected set (the four round-0 content gates plus the two new Shipped/Planned entries). All other 13 entries (anti-vacuity companions [3],[6],[8],[10],[14]; regression/official/regression-style guards [4],[11],[12],[15],[16],[17],[18],[19]) correctly PASS pre-implementation, as expected for checks that must hold both before and after implementation.

Contract 032.json (revision 1) is schema-valid: `python3 -c "jsonschema.validate(json.load(open('.agent/contracts/032.json')), json.load(open('schema/contract.schema.json')))"` exits 0, re-run independently as part of this review. `approvals.revisions` is 1 and `approvals.round0_review_ref` correctly points to `.agent/qa/032-contract-review.json`.

Repository left byte-for-byte unmodified by this review with respect to anything this feature's contract could touch: `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` before and after this round-1 review shows the same untracked paths for 032's own scope (`.agent/contracts/032.json`, `.agent/qa/032-contract-review.json`, this new `.agent/qa/032-contract-review-rev1.json`), plus the concurrently-executing feature 033's own legitimately-growing artifact set (`.agent/contracts/033.json`, `.agent/qa/033-contract-review.json`, `.agent/qa/033-contract-review-rev1.json`, fourteen `.agent/evidence/033-verify-*.txt` files, and `.agent/evidence/033/verification.txt`, all of which appeared during this review session as 033 progressed -- expected, out of 032's scope, never touched by this review) and the pre-existing untracked `v0.6.0-release-notes.md`. `ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md` is confirmed ABSENT at the time this review concludes (`ls ecosystem/` shows only `README.md`); the one reverted `touch`/`rm` pair used to simulate its landing left no residue. .agent/run_state.json was not read for mutation and was not edited by this review.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 1. All three round-0 findings are independently re-verified as closed, not merely trusted from the generator's design_notes.revision1_corrections claim: (1) the HIGH-severity gap in the structural NIZAM.json check (old verification[11], now [13]) is fixed via exact-list equality (`key_documents==['ecosystem/README.md']`) -- both round-0 kill-shot scratch fixtures (a dangling extra path, and the same extra path simulated as existing after a reverted 033-landing probe) now correctly FAIL, while a single-correct-entry control fixture still PASSES; (2) the LOW-severity gap in the version-bump check (old/new verification[5]/[6]) is fixed via a genuine semver-tuple comparison strictly greater than (0,1,0) -- the round-0 kill-shot 0.0.1-downgrade fixture now correctly FAILS, a genuine 0.2.0 bump correctly PASSES, and the anti-vacuity companion still correctly holds against HEAD; (3) the non-blocking unsupported-claim gap (the Shipped/Planned annotation commitment) is closed by new verification[9]/[10], a dynamically filesystem-aware check whose correctness was proven not just statically but via a live, reverted simulation of feature 033 landing ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md -- the same fixture's pass/fail verdict correctly flips before and after the simulated landing, and its anti-vacuity companion holds against HEAD. Structural diff confirms `scope` and `non_goals` are byte-identical to round 0, and exactly 14 of the (now 19) verification entries are unchanged verbatim -- only the three targeted entries were revised and two new entries added, exactly as claimed and nothing more. All 19 verification commands were re-extracted from the live contract and re-run from the repository root: the expected six content-gated entries correctly fail pre-implementation and the remaining 13 correctly pass. The contract is schema-valid against schema/contract.schema.json. The repository was left unmodified by this review with respect to 032's own scope (all scratch fixtures and the one live-tree touch/rm simulation were fully reverted and confirmed residue-free); feature 033's concurrent, out-of-scope artifact growth during this review session was observed and correctly left untouched. Cleared to advance to Loop-2 implementation.
